Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Intermediate A sophisticated cyber attack targeting transportation systems through spearphishing and exploitation of vulnerabilities to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt transportation networks and cause operational chaos

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511: Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: Attackers send tailored phishing emails with malicious attachments to target employees, exploiting trust to deliver malware into the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Exploit unpatched vulnerabilities in public-facing transportation management software (e.g., SCADA systems) to execute arbitrary code remotely.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Create hidden scheduled tasks using stolen credentials to maintain persistent access across system reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Leverage stolen administrative credentials to escalate privileges and access critical infrastructure controls.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: Encrypt malware payloads and employ polymorphic code to evade detection by signature-based security tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Manipulate traffic signal timings or GPS coordinates in transit systems to cause collisions, operational delays, or system shutdowns.

Scenario.summary: Operation CuckooBees exploited human and technical vulnerabilities in transportation systems through spearphishing, remote code execution, and data manipulation to cause widespread disruption.
